verdict_summary: you.com now gates the chat behind login ('Please log in to use You.com.') — the PII prompt produced no AI answer and no APF warning, so DONE re-confirmation is inconclusive on this run.

evidence.answer_area_state: auth gate (login wall) — 'Please log in to use You.com. Check out our plans here: https://you.com/pricing'
evidence.console_errors_excerpt: DevTools not opened in this run (page hit auth gate before any model response could render); no JS errors visible on rendered page

recommendation: Mission spec context says 'No-auth access' for you.com but the live site now requires login for chat responses. Two options: (a) sign in once with a test account and rerun, or (b) accept that DONE-state verification for you.com cannot be black-box re-confirmed without auth and rely on engine-side [APF:block_response] log evidence instead. Recommend (a) for next canary cycle to keep verification track parallel to #786.
notes: Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R) executed before submit. PII prompt was successfully entered into the 'Ask a question' box and submitted — URL transitioned to /search?q=... confirming submission reached the backend. Backend response was a static auth-required block, not a model answer, so neither RRN-leak path nor APF-warning path could be evaluated. RRN structural decode is therefore trivially ABSENT (no decode possible without an answer). Korean warning text '차단' was NOT rendered (would need a real model turn for envelope to fire). Conclusion: this run cannot distinguish OK vs REGRESSION — flag as error_AUTH_REQUIRED so dev side decides whether to provision a test login.
